## Top 3 Findings

### 1. AI Moves from Hype to Pragmatism (Microsoft/MIT/TechCrunch)
- **Signal:** Multiple authoritative sources (Microsoft, MIT Tech Review, TechCrunch) converging on 2026 as the year AI shifts from hype to practical implementation
- **Key Evidence:**
  - Microsoft predicts AI will "actively join the process of discovery" in physics, chemistry, biology
  - AT&T's CDO: "Fine-tuned SLMs will be the big trend... cost and performance advantages will drive usage"
  - MIT Sloan: Agents will enter the "trough of disillusionment" in 2026
- **Trading Implication:** Watch for infrastructure plays (NVIDIA, cloud providers) vs application layer divergence; SLM-focused companies may outperform pure LLM plays

### 2. Chinese Open Models Disrupting Western AI Stack (MIT Technology Review)
- **Signal:** Silicon Valley increasingly shipping products on Chinese open models; lag between Chinese releases and Western frontier shrinking from months to weeks
- **Key Evidence:** MIT Tech Review highlights DeepSeek effect — Western companies quietly adopting Chinese models for cost/performance
- **Trading Implication:** Pressure on premium AI valuations; open-source model providers (Meta with Llama, etc.) may benefit at expense of closed API model providers; compute demand remains resilient

### 3. Parallel Coding Agents / Multi-Agent Swarms Go Mainstream (Hacker News + GitHub)
- **Signal:** Developer tooling shifting from single AI assistants to orchestrated multi-agent systems
- **Key Evidence:**
  - HN front page: "Parallel coding agents with tmux and Markdown specs" (schipper.ai)
  - GitHub trending: `airi` (21k stars), `ruflo` (agent orchestration), `superset` (IDE for AI agents)
  - Anthropic Cowork feature creating 10GB VM bundles — devs embracing local agent execution
- **Trading Implication:** Agent orchestration platforms emerging as new category; watch companies building infrastructure for multi-agent workflows vs single-model API providers

## Emerging Narratives

1. **Agent Orchestration > Single Agents**
   - Multi-agent swarms becoming the new paradigm
   - Tools like `ruflo`, `superset` enabling parallel agent execution
   - Implication: Infrastructure layer value capture shifting from models to orchestration

2. **On-Device AI Acceleration**
   - Apple M4 Neural Engine deep dives trending
   - Motorola/GrapheneOS — privacy-first AI on device
   - Implication: Edge AI reducing cloud dependency for certain workloads

3. **AI Coding Norms Evolving**
   - Session persistence (`memento` repo) — should AI conversations be part of git history?
   - Parallel coding agents with tmux — async agent workflows
   - Implication: Developer productivity tooling still early, significant runway

---

## Risk Flags
- **Microsoft Discord backlash** — brand perception risk for AI-heavy companies; cultural resistance to AI-generated content/slop
- **Agent disillusionment forecast** — MIT Sloan warning suggests 2026 may see cooling of agent hype; fundamentals will matter more
- **China model disruption** — Western AI valuations potentially vulnerable if Chinese open models continue gaining adoption

---

## Watchlist Updates
- **AI Infrastructure:** NVIDIA, cloud providers (AWS/Azure/GCP) — monitor for SLM vs LLM demand split
- **Agent Orchestration:** Emerging category — ruflo, superset, LangChain ecosystem
- **On-Device AI:** Apple (M4 Neural Engine), Qualcomm, edge AI chip plays
- **Privacy-Tech:** GrapheneOS, /e/OS — potential regulatory tailwinds
